---
title = "Bibliography"
---

*Calepin* processes citations from BibTeX/BibLaTeX files using [hayagriva](https://github.com/typst/hayagriva). Specify one or more `.bib` files in the front matter, and citation keys in the document body are resolved automatically.


# Front matter

The `bibliography` field accepts a single path or a list. The optional `csl` field points to a [Citation Style Language](https://citationstyles.org/) file.

```yaml
---
title: My Document
bibliography: references.bib
csl: apa.csl
---
```

Multiple bibliography files are merged into a single library:

```yaml
---
bibliography:
  - refs_theory.bib
  - refs_empirical.bib
---
```


# Citation syntax

Three citation forms are supported:

| Syntax | Output | Description |
|--------|--------|-------------|
| `@key` | Author et al. (Year) | Narrative citation |
| `[@key]` | (Author et al. Year) | Parenthetical citation |
| `[-@key]` | Year | Suppress author, year only |
| `[@key1; @key2]` | (Author1 Year1; Author2 Year2) | Grouped citations |

Grouped references also work for cross-references: `[@fig-one; @fig-two]` produces linked references like [@fig-one; @fig-two].

These are compatible with Quarto and Pandoc syntax.


# CSL styles

By default, *Calepin* uses the Chicago author-date style. To customize citation formatting, set the `csl` field in the front matter or in `_calepin/config.toml`. The value can be a built-in style name or a file path:

```yaml
---
csl: apa                          # built-in name (no .csl extension)
csl: styles/my-journal.csl       # file path
---
```

Resolution order (first match wins):

1. File path --- If the `csl` value points to an existing `.csl` file, it is used directly.
2. Built-in name --- *Calepin* ships with hundreds of styles from the [hayagriva](https://github.com/typst/hayagriva) archive. Run `calepin extra csl` to list all available names.
3. Default --- If no `csl` field is set, *Calepin* falls back to `chicago-author-date`.

If the specified style is not found or is a dependent style, *Calepin* warns and falls back to the default.

Additional `.csl` files for journals not covered by the built-in archive are available from the [Zotero style repository](https://www.zotero.org/styles).

# References section

When a document contains at least one resolved citation, *Calepin* appends a References section at the end of the output automatically. Unresolved keys (those not found in any `.bib` file) are left as-is.


Two dummy figures so we can demonstrate cross-references:

::: {#fig-one}
[]{.placeholder width=100 height=100}

First figure
:::

::: {#fig-two}
[]{.placeholder width=100 height=100}

Second figure
:::
